title: Model Timeline
intro: When did which model arrive — and when does something disappear? The chronicle of major AI models: releases, sunsets, and price changes, each with a source.

eintraege.name: Claude Sonnet 5 — end of introductory pricing
eintraege.text: The introductory price of $2/$10 per million tokens ends; after that the regular price of $3/$15 applies.

eintraege.name: DeepSeek: old model names get migrated
eintraege.text: From this day, the old API names deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner point to V4-Flash — anyone using the old models should check their integration.

eintraege.name: Claude Fable 5 — permanent in Max & Team Premium
eintraege.text: From 20 July 2026 included in all Max and Team Premium plans (at 50% of limits). Pro and Team Standard keep access via usage credits and receive a one-time $100 credit. The previously announced sunset did not happen.

eintraege.name: Kimi K3
eintraege.text: 2.8 trillion-parameter MoE with 1M context — according to Moonshot the largest open model in the world; the weights follow on July 27, until then API-only.

eintraege.name: Grok 4.5
eintraege.text: Positioned for agentic coding, 500K context, $2/$6 per million tokens — the cheapest model in xAI's own comparison chart.

eintraege.name: Muse Spark 1.1 + Meta Model API
eintraege.text: Meta's first closed-weight frontier model, accessible via the new OpenAI-SDK-compatible Meta Model API (public preview).

eintraege.name: GPT-5.6 family (Sol, Terra, Luna)
eintraege.text: Three tiers: Sol as the flagship ($5/$30), Terra as the mid-range option ($2.50/$15), and Luna as the cheapest variant ($1/$6).

eintraege.name: Gemini CLI + Code Assist for GitHub discontinued
eintraege.text: Google is discontinuing Gemini CLI for free users; the successor is the Antigravity CLI. Code Assist for GitHub: deprecation starts June 18, full shutdown July 17.

eintraege.name: Qwen3.7-Max
eintraege.text: Alibaba's agentic flagship ("designed for the agent era") — proprietary for the first time instead of open-weight, with 1M context.

eintraege.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash (GA)
eintraege.text: According to Google, 4x faster output and ahead of its own 3.1 Pro on coding benchmarks — Flash is no longer a budget tier ($1.50/$9).

eintraege.name: GLM-4.7
eintraege.text: 358B MoE under the MIT license — an open-weights workhorse for agentic coding, with a free Flash variant.

eintraege.name: Mistral Large 3
eintraege.text: Europe's open-weights flagship: 675B MoE under Apache 2.0, with vision and an EU hosting option.
